question.text: In which year did the CBOT introduce the first exchange-traded derivative contracts (futures)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While CBOT was established in 1848 for forward trading, it was in 1865 that CBOT introduced the first exchange-traded futures contracts — standardized contracts that could be traded on the exchange. **The Trap:** 1848 is when CBOT was established (forward contracts). 1919 is when CME was formed. 1972 is when IMM (part of CME) launched currency futures. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII tests this historical progression from OTC forwards to exchange-traded futures. **Key Takeaway:** 1865 marks the birth of exchange-traded futures. The key innovation was standardization of contract terms, which distinguished futures from forwards.
